example: Please send us the original Bill of Lading once the vessel departs.
The Commercial Invoice must state the correct value of goods for customs purposes.
The Packing List shows the quantity, weight, and dimensions of each carton.
A Certificate of Origin is required to qualify for preferential tariff rates.
The consignee must present the Delivery Order to pick up cargo from the terminal.
Please submit your Shipping Instruction before the documentation cut-off date.
The carrier sends an Arrival Notice to the consignee when the vessel nears the port.
The Air Waybill number is used to track the shipment throughout its air journey.
A Seaway Bill is non-negotiable and the consignee does not need to present the original.
The buyer opened a Letter of Credit to guarantee payment upon shipment of goods.
The carrier must file the cargo manifest with customs before vessel arrival.
An accurate Customs Declaration is essential to avoid delays at the border.
The bank requires an Insurance Certificate as part of the L/C document set.
The buyer requested an Inspection Certificate to confirm the goods meet specifications.
A Fumigation Certificate is required for wooden packaging entering certain countries.
Food exports require a Health Certificate issued by the relevant authority.
The Weight Certificate confirmed the net and gross weights of the shipment.
The Quality Certificate verified that the goods conform to the agreed standards.
Please review the Draft Bill of Lading and confirm the details before we issue the original.
The shipper requested a Telex Release so the consignee could collect cargo without the original B/L.
Three originals of the Original Bill of Lading were issued as a full set.
The freight forwarder issued a House Bill of Lading for each individual shipper in the LCL consolidation.
The Master Bill of Lading is issued by the ocean carrier to the freight forwarder.
A Through Bill of Lading covers the entire journey including inland transportation.
The bank insisted on a Clean Bill of Lading with no remarks about damaged cargo.
The terminal requires a Shipping Order before loading cargo onto the vessel.
We received the Booking Confirmation from the carrier with the vessel and voyage details.
Please settle the Freight Invoice within 30 days of the vessel departure.
A Debit Note was issued for the additional storage charges incurred.
The carrier issued a Credit Note after the overcharged freight fee was confirmed.
The buyer used the Proforma Invoice to apply for an import license before shipment.
A Dangerous Goods Declaration must be submitted before shipping hazardous materials.
The Safety Data Sheet provides handling and emergency response information for chemicals.
The Container Load Plan details how each carton is arranged inside the container.
A Damage Report was filed immediately after the broken goods were discovered at the warehouse.
The insurer required a Survey Report from a certified surveyor to process the claim.
The consignee signed a Letter of Indemnity to obtain cargo release without the original B/L.
Certain controlled goods require a valid Import License before customs clearance.
Dual-use items need an Export License approved by the relevant government authority.
The importer arranged a Pre-shipment Inspection to verify product quality at the factory.
The Certificate of Analysis confirmed the chemical composition meets regulatory requirements.
All wooden pallets and plant-based goods must be accompanied by a Phytosanitary Certificate.
Live animals and animal products require a Veterinary Certificate from an accredited authority.
GSP Form A allows developing countries to export goods at reduced tariff rates to qualifying markets.
Some countries require a Consular Invoice certified by their local consulate.
The exporter drew a Bill of Exchange on the buyer as part of the documentary collection.
The trucker obtained a Dock Receipt when delivering cargo to the container terminal.
The Mate's Receipt is issued by the ship's officer acknowledging cargo received on board.
The Warehouse Receipt serves as proof that goods are stored at the designated facility.
The signed Proof of Delivery confirms the consignee received the shipment in good condition.
The Electronic Bill of Lading reduces paper handling and speeds up the documentation process.
A Cargo Receipt was issued when the freight forwarder accepted the goods at the warehouse.
The driver obtained a Delivery Receipt with the consignee's signature upon final delivery.
The carrier issued a Freight Release after all outstanding charges were settled.
The shipper signed an endorsement on the back of the B/L to transfer title to the buyer.
An original Bill of Lading is a Negotiable Document that can be transferred by endorsement.
The Non-negotiable Copy is for reference only and cannot be used to claim cargo.
The Stowage Plan shows the position of each container on board the vessel.
The tally clerk recorded each piece of cargo on the Tally Sheet during loading.
The Cargo Manifest lists all shipments on board and is filed with customs upon arrival.
The shipper requested an Amendment to correct the consignee's address on the B/L.
The importer issued a Letter of Authorization to the broker for customs clearance.
The company signed a Power of Attorney granting the freight forwarder legal authority to act on its behalf.
A Bank Guarantee was required to secure the release of goods held by customs.
The supplier provided a Performance Bond to guarantee timely delivery of the contract goods.
Exporters can use the RCEP Certificate to enjoy reduced tariffs within RCEP member countries.
Textile exporters must submit a Quota Certificate to comply with import restrictions.
The Temperature Record from the reefer container confirmed the cold chain was maintained.
The Seal Certificate records the seal number applied to the container after stuffing.
The exporter provided a Non-wooden Packing Declaration to certify no wooden materials were used.
The contract required notarization by a certified notary public before it was legally valid.
Documents destined for Hague Convention countries must carry an Apostille stamp.
Legalization by the importing country's embassy is required for certain trade documents.
A Combined Transport Document covers cargo movements by more than one mode of transport.
A Straight Bill of Lading names a specific consignee and is non-negotiable.
An Order Bill of Lading can be transferred to another party through endorsement.
A Foul Bill of Lading is issued when the carrier notes visible damage or shortage of cargo.
An On Board Bill of Lading confirms that cargo has been physically loaded onto the named vessel.
Express Release allows the consignee to pick up cargo without presenting original documents.
After surrendering the original B/L at origin, the cargo can be released at destination without it.
A Blank Endorsement allows the bearer of the B/L to claim the cargo without further formalities.
The Freight Prepaid Stamp on the B/L confirms that ocean freight has been paid by the shipper.
The Freight Collect Stamp indicates the consignee is responsible for paying the freight charges.
The L/C requires a full set of original Bills of Lading to be presented.
The Copy Not Negotiable is stamped on extra prints of the B/L issued for reference only.
A Manufacturer's Affidavit is used to certify the country of origin when a formal certificate is unavailable.
The exporter signed a Country of Origin Declaration stating that goods were made in China.
A Multimodal Transport Document is issued when cargo travels by sea, rail, and road under one contract.
The importer submitted an Anti-dumping Certificate to prove the goods were not sold below fair market value.
Some importing countries require a Radiation Certificate to confirm goods are free from contamination.
A Free Sale Certificate confirms that the product is legally sold in the country of manufacture.

example: The ISPM 15 Certificate proves that wooden pallets have been heat-treated or fumigated.

The Supplier Declaration confirms the origin of materials used in the manufactured goods.
The Test Report from an accredited lab confirmed the product meets safety standards.
The Cargo Insurance Certificate must cover at least 110% of the CIF value of the shipment.
The Packing Declaration certifies that packaging materials comply with importing country regulations.
The Single Window Declaration allows traders to submit all customs documents through one portal.
The carrier issued a Release Order authorizing the terminal to hand over the cargo.
The importer presented a Shipping Guarantee to obtain cargo before the original B/L arrived.
The factory stuffed 500 cartons into a single 40-foot Container.
The Vessel is scheduled to depart from Shanghai on March 15th.
The Port of Loading is Ningbo, and the cargo is expected to be shipped next week.
Los Angeles is the Port of Discharge for this transpacific shipment.
Use our online Cargo Tracking system to check the real-time status of your shipment.
Customs Clearance at the destination port took three days due to document issues.
The Freight Rate from Shenzhen to Hamburg has risen sharply this quarter.
Please make a Booking with the carrier at least one week before the cut-off date.
The Transit Time from Guangzhou to Rotterdam is approximately 28 days.
The shipment qualified for FCL as it filled an entire 40-foot container.
Since the cargo volume is small, we recommend shipping it as LCL to save cost.
We booked two 20GP containers for this shipment of machinery parts.
A 40GP container can hold approximately 26 tons of cargo.
We upgraded to a 40HQ for its extra cubic capacity to accommodate the bulky goods.
Fresh seafood must be shipped in a Reefer Container set to minus 18 degrees Celsius.
The marble slabs were loaded from the top using an Open Top Container.
The industrial machinery was loaded on a Flat Rack Container due to its oversized dimensions.
Liquid chemicals are transported in a Tank Container to prevent leakage.
The cargo will be routed via Transshipment at Singapore before arriving in Colombo.
We prefer Direct Sailing to avoid the risk of delays at transshipment ports.
A Feeder Vessel collects cargo from smaller ports and transfers it to the hub port.
The Mother Vessel operates the main ocean route between Asia and Europe.
The empty container must be returned to the Container Yard within the free time allowed.
LCL shipments are consolidated at the Container Freight Station before being stuffed into a container.
Goods stored in a Bonded Warehouse are not subject to import duties until they enter the market.
Stuffing of the container is scheduled for Monday morning at the factory.
Devanning will be carried out at the destination warehouse once the container arrives.
The cargo must arrive at the port before the Cut-off Date to catch the scheduled vessel.
Please check the Sailing Schedule to find the next available departure from Tianjin.
The ETD from Qingdao is confirmed as March 20th pending weather conditions.
The ETA at the Port of Long Beach is April 15th based on current vessel speed.
Port Congestion at the destination has delayed vessel arrival by five days.
Due to vessel overbooking, our shipment suffered a Cargo Rollover to the following week.
The Shipper is responsible for preparing the cargo and providing accurate documentation.
The Consignee must present the original B/L to collect the cargo at the destination port.
The Notify Party will be contacted when the vessel arrives at the destination port.
The Freight Forwarder arranged the booking, documentation, and customs clearance for the shipment.
We hired a licensed Customs Broker to handle the import declaration at the port of entry.
As an NVOCC, the freight forwarder issues its own house bills of lading to shippers.
The THC covers the cost of moving containers within the port terminal.
Rising oil prices led to an increase in the BAF applied to all ocean freight rates.
The CAF is applied to compensate carriers for exchange rate fluctuations.
Carriers impose a Peak Season Surcharge during the busy pre-Christmas shipping period.
The carrier announced a GRI of USD 500 per container effective from the first of next month.
The importer was charged Demurrage because the container was not picked up within the free time.
Detention charges apply when the container is held outside the terminal beyond the allowed period.
The carrier allows seven days of Free Time before demurrage charges begin to accrue.
Under CIF terms, the shipper arranges Prepaid Freight on behalf of the buyer.
Under FOB terms, the consignee pays Collect Freight directly to the carrier.
The All-in Rate quoted by the carrier includes ocean freight and all applicable surcharges.
Ocean Freight from China to Europe has been volatile due to Red Sea disruptions.
Air Freight is significantly more expensive but offers much shorter transit times.
The Door to Door service covers pickup from the supplier's factory to the buyer's warehouse.
Port to Port service means the carrier is responsible only between the two named seaports.
We strongly recommend purchasing Cargo Insurance to cover potential transit risks.
Marine Insurance protects cargo against loss or damage during ocean transport.
When the vessel ran aground, all cargo owners shared the General Average losses proportionally.
The carrier invoked Force Majeure after a typhoon disrupted the sailing schedule.
The wind turbine blades are classified as Out of Gauge and require special flat rack containers.
Hazardous Cargo must be declared and shipped according to IMDG code regulations.
The container was flagged as Overweight at the gate and had to be re-stuffed.
The Gross Weight of the shipment includes the weight of goods and all packaging materials.
The Net Weight refers to the weight of the goods only, excluding any packaging.
The Tare Weight of the empty container is stamped on its door and must be accounted for.
For air freight, the Chargeable Weight is whichever is greater: actual or volumetric weight.
The Volumetric Weight of the light but bulky package exceeded its actual weight.
The total volume of this LCL shipment is 8 Cubic Meters.
Please provide the Measurement of each carton to calculate the total CBM.
All cartons must be Palletized before delivery to the container freight station.
Steel pipes and lumber are often shipped as Breakbulk cargo on conventional vessels.
New automobiles are typically transported by RoRo vessels and driven on and off the ship.
A Multimodal shipment from Chengdu to Germany uses rail across Central Asia then sea.
Cross-border e-commerce has driven strong demand for parcel logistics services.
Inland Transportation from the factory to the port is arranged by the shipper under FOB terms.
Last Mile delivery is the most expensive part of the supply chain due to urban traffic.
The freight forwarder handles Consolidation of multiple small shipments into one container.
After arrival, Deconsolidation is performed at the CFS to separate individual LCL shipments.
AMS Filing must be submitted to US Customs at least 24 hours before loading at origin.
ISF Filing is required by US Customs 24 hours prior to vessel departure for all imports.
The Spot Rate has increased significantly due to the current tight market capacity.
Our Contract Rate with the carrier is fixed for 12 months to ensure cost stability.
The carrier exercised its Carrier's Lien and held the cargo until freight charges were paid.
The Salvage team recovered containers from the stranded vessel before it capsized.
The captain ordered Jettison of several containers to stabilize the vessel during the storm.
The Particular Average claim was filed after a portion of the cargo was damaged by seawater.
An additional War Risk premium was added for vessels transiting through conflict zones.
Proper Stowage of hazardous goods is critical to ensure vessel and crew safety.
Steel wires and chains were used for Lashing to secure the machinery on the flat rack.
Dunnage such as wooden planks and airbags was placed around the cargo to prevent shifting.
A Heavy Lift crane was deployed to load the transformer onto the vessel.
Haulage from the inland depot to the port was arranged by the logistics company.
Drayage services move containers between the port terminal and the nearby distribution center.
Cartage charges apply for transporting cargo within the local port area.
Several surcharges including THC, BAF, and PSS were added to the base freight rate.
The Base Rate is the fundamental ocean freight charge before any surcharges are applied.
All Risk Coverage provides the broadest protection against physical loss or damage to cargo.
Priority Booking guarantees space on the next available vessel during peak seasons.
A Vessel Delay of four days was caused by severe weather in the Pacific Ocean.
The Intermodal solution combines rail freight from Chicago to the West Coast seaport.
Global disruptions have highlighted the need for resilient Supply Chain management.
Our price is USD 50,000 FOB Shanghai, excluding freight and insurance.
The CIF price includes cost, insurance, and freight to the destination port.
Under EXW terms, the buyer is responsible for all transportation from our factory.
We quote DDP Hamburg, meaning all duties and taxes are included in our price.
The goods will be delivered DAP buyer's warehouse; import clearance is the buyer's responsibility.
Under FCA terms, risk transfers to the buyer once we hand goods to the carrier.
Our CFR Rotterdam price is USD 12,000 per container, insurance excluded.
Please confirm which Incoterms apply to this shipment before we finalize the contract.
The current tariff on imported steel is 25%, which significantly affects our costs.
Import duty must be paid before customs will release the cargo.
Under CIP terms, the seller pays freight and insurance to the named destination.
The CPT price covers transportation to the buyer's city, but not insurance.
DPU requires the seller to unload the goods at the named place of destination.
Under FAS terms, our obligation ends when goods are placed alongside the vessel.
The import duty rate for this HS code is 8%, payable upon entry.
China levies an export duty on certain raw materials to restrict outbound supply.
The EU imposed a 48% anti-dumping duty on Chinese solar panel imports.
Countervailing duties are applied to offset subsidies granted by foreign governments.
A safeguard duty was imposed temporarily to protect domestic producers from surging imports.
The MFN rate for this product is 6.5%, applicable to all WTO member countries.
Goods from least-developed countries may qualify for zero duty under GSP.
Under the FTA between the two countries, most goods are traded at zero tariff.

example: RCEP has created one of the world's largest free trade areas in the Asia-Pacific region.
With a valid certificate of origin, you can apply for the preferential tariff rate.
Please confirm the HS code for this product so we can determine the applicable duty rate.
The customs value is generally based on the transaction value stated on the commercial invoice.
The transaction value method is the primary method for customs valuation under WTO rules.
The declared value on the invoice must match the actual transaction price.
Import VAT of 13% will be charged on the customs value plus import duty.
Excise duty applies to specific goods such as tobacco, alcohol, and petroleum products.
The annual import quota for this product category has been fully utilized.
A trade embargo prevents any commercial transactions with the sanctioned country.
We must screen all customers against the sanctions list before accepting any order.
The trade balance between the two countries has been in surplus for five consecutive years.
China's trade surplus with the US reached a record high last quarter.
The country's trade deficit widened as import growth outpaced export growth.
We offer open account payment terms of net 60 days to trusted long-term customers.
For new customers, we require cash in advance before we begin production.
Documentary collection provides more security than open account but less than a letter of credit.
Under D/P terms, documents are released to the buyer only upon payment.
D/A terms allow the buyer to receive documents upon accepting the draft for future payment.
Please arrange T/T payment of USD 25,000 to our bank account within 5 working days.
Payment is by sight LC, meaning funds are released as soon as documents are presented.
The buyer requested a 90-day usance LC to allow time to sell the goods before payment.
The supplier requires a standby LC as security in case the buyer defaults on payment.
Please proceed with production upon receipt of the signed purchase order.
Both parties must sign the sales contract before the order is confirmed.
We received their counter offer at USD 18 per unit, but our floor price is USD 20.
Upon acceptance of the draft, the buyer is obligated to pay at maturity.
Please find attached the proforma invoice for your review and approval.
Our latest price list is valid until the end of this quarter.
Thank you for your inquiry; please find our competitive quotation attached.
Our firm offer is valid for 7 days, subject to prior sale.
Any disputes shall be resolved by arbitration in Singapore under SIAC rules.
The contract includes a dispute resolution clause specifying mediation before litigation.
Liquidated damages of 0.5% per day apply for late delivery under the contract.
All products come with a 12-month warranty against manufacturing defects.
The supplier shall provide indemnity against any claims arising from product defects.
Our total liability under this contract shall not exceed the contract value.
Under FOB terms, risk transfer occurs when goods pass over the ship's rail.
Title to the goods passes to the buyer only upon full payment of the invoice.
We offer consignment arrangements where payment is made only after goods are sold.
Our distribution agreement grants exclusive rights to sell in the ASEAN region.
We can offer exclusivity in your market if you commit to a minimum annual purchase volume.
All intellectual property rights in the product design remain with the seller.
The trademark is registered in over 50 countries to protect our brand globally.
This technology is protected by a patent, so any unauthorized use would be infringement.
All product manuals and artwork are protected by copyright and may not be reproduced.
We seized a large shipment of counterfeit goods bearing our registered trademark.
Manufacturers should carry product liability insurance to cover injury claims.
Our factory holds ISO 9001 certification, ensuring consistent quality management.
All electrical products exported to Europe must carry CE marking to enter the market.
Our food packaging materials have received FDA approval for use in the US market.
Compliance with local regulations is a prerequisite for market entry.
Please confirm that the product meets all regulatory requirements in the destination country.

Customers who purchase over 500 units quarterly are eligible for a 2% rebate.
We can offer a 5% discount for orders exceeding 1,000 units.
The distributor applies a 30% markup over our wholesale price.
After all deductions, our net margin on this deal is only 8%.
Our MOQ for this product is 500 units per order.
The current lead time for production is 4 weeks from receipt of your purchase order.
Our standard payment terms are 30% deposit and 70% balance before shipment.
The delivery terms and conditions are clearly stated in Article 5 of the contract.
The buyer's bank issued a letter of guarantee to secure the seller's performance.
We can offer deferred payment of up to 90 days for established accounts.
Payment will be made in three equal installments over six months.
An advance payment of 30% is required to confirm the order and start production.
The balance payment of 70% is due upon presentation of the shipping documents.
Please arrange wire transfer to our USD account; details are provided on the invoice.
The contract is settled in USD to avoid exchange rate risk for both parties.
We use forward contracts to hedge against currency risk on large export deals.
The price clause specifies that costs shall be adjusted quarterly based on raw material indices.
WTO members are obliged to grant most favored nation status to all other members.
We entered a forward contract to lock in the USD/EUR exchange rate for six months.
Hedging our currency exposure has reduced the volatility of our profit margins.
The penalty clause imposes a 1% fee per week for deliveries beyond the agreed schedule.
The agency agreement grants the partner the right to sign contracts on our behalf.
The force majeure clause excuses both parties from obligations due to natural disasters.
The contractor provided a performance guarantee equal to 10% of the contract value.
